message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker and update SESSION_TODO

- Issue tracker updated with latest bead status
- SESSION_TODO.md adds section 0 for bd-3bmd (joined-table
  inheritance DML: explicit WHERE/SET + ON CONFLICT) with
  detailed checklist for UPDATE, DELETE, INSERT ON CONFLICT,
  RETURNING, integration tests, and quality gates

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ak","title":"EPIC: ORM Relationship System","description":"# ORM Relationship System

## Overview
Implement full ORM relationship support comparable to SQLAlchemy/SQLModel. This is THE defining feature that separates an ORM from a mere query builder with type mapping.

## Why This Matters
Without relationships, users must:
- Manually write JOINs for every related query
- Manually track foreign keys
- Lose the core ergonomic benefit of an ORM (`hero.team` should just work)

Python SQLModel provides:
```python
class Hero(SQLModel, table=True):
    team_id: Optional[int] = Field(foreign_key="team.id")
    team: Optional["Team"] = Relationship(back_populates="heroes")

class Team(SQLModel, table=True):
    heroes: List["Hero"] = Relationship(back_populates="team")

# Usage: hero.team automatically fetches the related Team
```

## Rust Design Philosophy
Instead of runtime reflection magic, we use:
1. **Compile-time type safety** via proc macros
2. **Explicit relationship definitions** that generate correct code
3. **Optional lazy loading** (user opts in, not default)
4. **Eager loading support** for performance-conscious queries

## Target API (Rust)
```rust
#[derive(Model)]
#[sqlmodel(table = "heroes")]
struct Hero {
    #[sqlmodel(primary_key, auto_increment)]
    id: Option<i64>,
    
    #[sqlmodel(foreign_key = "teams.id")]
    team_id: Option<i64>,
    
    #[sqlmodel(relationship(model = "Team", foreign_key = "team_id"))]
    team: Related<Team>,  // or Lazy<Team> for lazy loading
}

#[derive(Model)]
#[sqlmodel(table = "teams")]
struct Team {
    #[sqlmodel(primary_key, auto_increment)]
    id: Option<i64>,
    
    name: String,
    
    #[sqlmodel(relationship(model = "Hero", back_populates = "team"))]
    heroes: RelatedMany<Hero>,
}
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] One-to-many relationships work (Team has many Heroes)
- [ ] Many-to-one relationships work (Hero belongs to Team)
- [ ] Many-to-many with link tables work
- [ ] Eager loading with JOINs works
- [ ] Optional lazy loading works
- [ ] back_populates synchronization works
- [ ] Cascade operations work via FK constraints
- [ ] Type-safe at compile time

## Architecture Decisions
1. **Related<T> vs Lazy<T>**: Two wrapper types - Related for eager-loaded, Lazy for deferred
2. **No automatic lazy loading by default**: Explicit is better for Rust, prevents N+1 surprises
3. **Macro-generated accessors**: The relationship macro generates the fetch methods
4. **Connection context required**: Must pass connection/session to load related data

## Dependencies
- Core Model trait (done)
- Query builder with JOINs (done)
- Session/Unit-of-Work (separate epic, can work without but better with)

## Estimated Scope
- 8-12 subtasks
- Touches: sqlmodel-core, sqlmodel-macros, sqlmodel-query
- New types: Related<T>, RelatedMany<T>, Lazy<T>, RelationshipInfo","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:12:54.238244618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:04:07.379475967Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:04:07.379414012Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1bt4","title":"Write comprehensive conversion boundary tests for Value type safety","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:35:16.270980935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:32:10.443761460Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:32:10.443680780Z","close_reason":"Added 17 comprehensive conversion boundary tests covering u64, f32, f64 precision, error messages, roundtrips, and negative boundaries","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bt4","depends_on_id":"bd-1epw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bt4","depends_on_id":"bd-21wb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bt4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ed","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bt4","depends_on_id":"bd-9a25","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-1bt4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What

Write comprehensive unit tests verifying that all numeric Value conversions either succeed correctly or fail with clear errors — covering the u64 overflow fix, f32 precision fix, and i64→f64 precision fix.

## Where

File: crates/sqlmodel-core/src/value.rs (extend existing test module)
File: crates/sqlmodel-core/src/row.rs (extend existing test module)

## Unit Tests Required

### u64 Overflow Tests (value.rs)
1. `test_u64_zero_converts` — `0u64.try_into::<Value>()` → Ok(BigInt(0))
2. `test_u64_max_i64_converts` — `(i64::MAX as u64).try_into()` → Ok(BigInt(i64::MAX))
3. `test_u64_over_i64_max_errors` — `(i64::MAX as u64 + 1).try_into()` → Err with clear message
4. `test_u64_max_errors` — `u64::MAX.try_into()` → Err
5. `test_u64_error_message_includes_value` — verify the error message contains the actual u64 value for debugging
6. `test_u64_boundary_i64_max_minus_one` — `(i64::MAX as u64 - 1).try_into()` → Ok

### f32 Precision Tests (value.rs)
7. `test_f64_to_f32_small_value_ok` — `Value::Double(1.5).try_into::<f32>()` → Ok(1.5)
8. `test_f64_to_f32_zero_ok` — `Value::Double(0.0).try_into::<f32>()` → Ok(0.0)
9. `test_f64_to_f32_large_value_errors` — `Value::Double(f64::MAX).try_into::<f32>()` → Err
10. `test_f64_to_f32_precision_loss_errors` — `Value::Double(1.0000000000000002).try_into::<f32>()` → Err (if precision lost)
11. `test_i32_to_f32_small_ok` — `Value::Int(1000).try_into::<f32>()` → Ok(1000.0)
12. `test_i32_to_f32_exact_boundary_ok` — `Value::Int(16_777_216).try_into::<f32>()` → Ok (2^24 is exact)
13. `test_i32_to_f32_over_boundary_errors` — `Value::Int(16_777_217).try_into::<f32>()` → Err (2^24+1 loses precision)
14. `test_i64_to_f32_large_errors` — `Value::BigInt(i64::MAX).try_into::<f32>()` → Err
15. `test_f32_error_message_is_descriptive` — verify error includes the value and explains precision loss

### i64→f64 Precision Tests (value.rs)
16. `test_i64_to_f64_small_ok` — `Value::BigInt(42).try_into::<f64>()` → Ok(42.0)
17. `test_i64_to_f64_exact_boundary_ok` — `Value::BigInt(1i64 << 53).try_into::<f64>()` → Ok (2^53 is exact in f64)
18. `test_i64_to_f64_over_boundary_errors` — `Value::BigInt((1i64 << 53) + 1).try_into::<f64>()` → Err
19. `test_i64_to_f64_negative_boundary_ok` — `Value::BigInt(-(1i64 << 53)).try_into::<f64>()` → Ok
20. `test_i64_to_f64_negative_over_boundary_errors` — `Value::BigInt(-(1i64 << 53) - 1).try_into::<f64>()` → Err
21. `test_i64_to_f64_max_errors` — `Value::BigInt(i64::MAX).try_into::<f64>()` → Err
22. `test_i64_to_f64_min_errors` — `Value::BigInt(i64::MIN).try_into::<f64>()` → Err (MIN = -2^63, exceeds 2^53)

### Row FromValue Tests (row.rs — mirrors value.rs tests)
23. `test_row_f32_from_large_double_errors` — verify FromValue for f32 from a Double row value errors
24. `test_row_f32_from_small_double_ok` — verify small values work
25. `test_row_f64_from_large_bigint_errors` — verify FromValue for f64 from a BigInt row value errors
26. `test_row_f64_from_small_bigint_ok` — verify small values work

### Roundtrip Tests
27. `test_u64_roundtrip_within_range` — u64 → Value → i64 → verify equal for values in range
28. `test_f32_roundtrip_preserves_value` — f32 → Value::Float → f32 → verify bitwise equal
29. `test_f64_i64_roundtrip` — i64 (small) → Value → f64 → verify equal

### Error Type Tests
30. `test_conversion_errors_are_type_errors` — verify all conversion failures return Error::Type variant
31. `test_conversion_errors_include_expected_type` — verify error has the target type name
32. `test_conversion_errors_include_actual_value` — verify error has the source value description

## Logging / Diagnostics

Each test should use descriptive assertion messages:
```rust
assert!(result.is_err(), "u64::MAX ({}) should not silently convert to i64", u64::MAX);
let err = result.unwrap_err();
assert!(
    err.to_string().contains("18446744073709551615"),
    "Error message should include the actual value, got: {}",
    err
);
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- At least 30 tests covering all conversion paths and boundary conditions
- Every error path tested with message content verification
- row.rs and value.rs both covered
- Tests document WHY each boundary value matters (comments)
- All tests pass with `cargo test -p sqlmodel-core`
- cargo clippy clean
","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:35:16Z"},{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-1bt4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## UPDATE: u64 tests must reflect corrected approach (see bd-1epw latest comment)

Since From<u64> is being REMOVED (not kept), the u64 tests should be:

### u64 Strict Path (TryFrom)
1. `test_u64_zero_try_into` — `0u64.try_into::<Value>()` → Ok(BigInt(0))
2. `test_u64_max_i64_try_into` — `(i64::MAX as u64).try_into()` → Ok(BigInt(i64::MAX))
3. `test_u64_over_i64_max_try_into_errors` — `(i64::MAX as u64 + 1).try_into()` → Err
4. `test_u64_max_try_into_errors` — `u64::MAX.try_into()` → Err
5. `test_u64_error_message_includes_value` — verify error contains the actual u64 value
6. `test_u64_boundary_minus_one` — `(i64::MAX as u64 - 1).try_into()` → Ok

### u64 Lenient Path (from_u64_clamped)
7. `test_u64_clamped_in_range` — `Value::from_u64_clamped(42)` → BigInt(42)
8. `test_u64_clamped_over_max` — `Value::from_u64_clamped(u64::MAX)` → BigInt(i64::MAX)
9. `test_u64_clamped_at_boundary` — `Value::from_u64_clamped(i64::MAX as u64 + 1)` → BigInt(i64::MAX)

### Reverse path (Value → u64)
10. `test_negative_bigint_to_u64_errors` — `Value::BigInt(-1).try_into::<u64>()` → Err
11. `test_zero_bigint_to_u64` — `Value::BigInt(0).try_into::<u64>()` → Ok(0)

Replace original tests 1-6 with these 11 tests.
","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:51:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1cbm","title":"Joined-table inheritance bulk insert (insert_many)","description":"## Problem

`insert_many!` currently assumes a single table. Joined-table inheritance children require inserting into the base table and then the child table, preserving base-generated PKs and cancel-correct transactional semantics.

## Goals
- Add joined-inheritance aware bulk insert for joined children.
- Ensure deterministic PK propagation (including auto-increment single-PK).
- Keep dialect-correct semantics (SQLite column omission for defaults, MySQL placeholder rules, Postgres RETURNING).

## Acceptance
- [ ] `insert_many!(&[Child])` inserts base+child rows correctly
- [ ] Handles empty input and mixed-default cases
- [ ] Tests for SQL batching behavior and SQLite integration test

## Goals

- Ensure deterministic PK propagation (including auto-increment single-PK).
- Keep dialect-correct semantics (SQLite column omission for defaults, MySQL placeholder rules, Postgres RETURNING).

 {"id":"bd-1cr","title":"Field Attributes: Complete All 40+ Parameters","description":"## Overview

Implement ALL Field() parameters from Python SQLModel/Pydantic.

## Python Field() Parameters (Complete List)

### Basic Parameters
- default: Any - Default value for field
- default_factory: Optional[Callable] - Factory function for defaults
- alias: Optional[str] - Input/output alias
- validation_alias: Optional[str] - Input-only alias (Pydantic v2)
- serialization_alias: Optional[str] - Output-only alias (Pydantic v2)
- title: Optional[str] - Field title in schema
- description: Optional[str] - Field description in schema
- exclude: Union[set, Mapping] - Exclude from model operations
- include: Union[set, Mapping] - Include in model operations
- schema_extra: Optional[dict] - Extra schema configuration
- const: Optional[bool] - Constant value constraint
- repr: bool - Include in repr (default: True)

### Validation Parameters
- gt, ge, lt, le: Optional[float] - Numeric bounds
- multiple_of: Optional[float] - Must be multiple of value
- max_digits: Optional[int] - Max decimal digits
- decimal_places: Optional[int] - Decimal precision
- min_items, max_items: Optional[int] - Collection size bounds
- unique_items: Optional[bool] - Items must be unique
- min_length, max_length: Optional[int] - String length bounds
- allow_mutation: bool - Allow field mutation
- regex: Optional[str] - Regex pattern validation
- discriminator: Optional[str] - Union type discrimination

### SQLAlchemy Column Parameters
- primary_key, nullable, unique, index - Column constraints
- sa_type - SQLAlchemy type override
- sa_column - Full SQLAlchemy Column (mutually exclusive with other SA params)
- sa_column_args, sa_column_kwargs - Extra Column() args/kwargs
- foreign_key, ondelete, onupdate - Foreign key config

## Current Status

Many are implemented but gaps exist in:
- Alias system (validation_alias, serialization_alias)
- Schema metadata (title, description, schema_extra)
- Collection validators (min_items, max_items, unique_items)
- Full sa_column override support
- multiple_of validation
- discriminator support

## Implementation Notes

Each parameter must:
1. Be parseable from #[sqlmodel(...)] or #[field(...)] attributes
2. Generate appropriate SQL DDL where applicable
3. Generate validation code where applicable
4. Work with serde for serialization
5. Have tests covering all edge cases","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:59:11.095624519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T13:02:22.502871496Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T13:02:22.502805383Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cr","depends_on_id":"bd-162","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1cuq","title":"Implement horizontal sharding","description":"## Description

Partition data across multiple databases.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlalchemy.ext.horizontal_shard import ShardedSession

class ShardedDB:
    def shard_chooser(self, mapper, instance, clause=None):
        if instance:
            return f'shard_{instance.region}'
        return 'shard_default'
    
    def id_chooser(self, query, ident):
        return ['shard_us', 'shard_eu']  # Search all
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
pub struct ShardedPool {
    shards: HashMap<String, Pool>,
    chooser: Box<dyn ShardChooser>,
}

pub trait ShardChooser: Send + Sync {
    fn choose_for_insert<M: Model>(&self, model: &M) -> String;
    fn choose_for_query(&self, query: &Query) -> Vec<String>;
}

// Example: shard by user_id modulo
struct ModuloShardChooser {
    shard_count: usize,
}

impl ShardChooser for ModuloShardChooser {
    fn choose_for_insert<M: Model>(&self, model: &M) -> String {
        let id = model.shard_key();
        format!("shard_{}", id % self.shard_count)
    }
}
```

## Shard Key

Models need shard key:
```rust
#[derive(Model)]
#[sqlmodel(shard_key = "user_id")]
struct Order {
    user_id: i64,
    // ...
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ShardedPool implementation
- [ ] ShardChooser trait
- [ ] Shard key on models
- [ ] Cross-shard queries
- [ ] Shard-aware transactions

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-core/src/sharding.rs)
- [ ] Test shard key extraction
- [ ] Test shard routing function
- [ ] Test cross-shard query handling
- [ ] Test shard configuration

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/horizontal_sharding.rs)
- [ ] Query routes to correct shard
- [ ] Insert routes by shard key
- [ ] Cross-shard aggregation
- [ ] Shard rebalancing
- [ ] Shard failover

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: Shard routing decision
- [ ] INFO: Shard selection
- [ ] WARN: Cross-shard query detected
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:12:30.646644148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:46:25.847264322Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:46:25.847125413Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cuq","depends_on_id":"bd-3lz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-1cuq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented core horizontal sharding infrastructure:

**Shard Key Support (Model derive macro):**
- Added shard_key attribute parsing in parse.rs
- Added SHARD_KEY constant to Model trait
- Added shard_key_value() method to Model trait
- Generated code handles both optional and non-optional shard key fields
- 5 integration tests in sqlmodel crate

**ShardedPool & ShardChooser (sqlmodel-pool crate):**
- Created sharding.rs module with ShardChooser trait
- Implemented ModuloShardChooser for numeric keys
- Implemented ShardedPool with multi-shard routing
- QueryHints for scatter-gather queries
- ShardedPoolStats for aggregate statistics
- 13 unit tests for sharding module

Remaining work:
- Cross-shard query execution (scatter-gather)
- Shard-aware transactions
- E2E tests with actual databases","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:45:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1cy","title":"Implement Window Functions (OVER clause)","description":"## Description

Support window functions with OVER clause.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlalchemy import func, over

# Row number
stmt = select(
    User.name,
    func.row_number().over(order_by=User.created_at).label('row_num')
)

# Partition by
stmt = select(
    Order.id,
    func.sum(Order.amount).over(partition_by=Order.customer_id).label('customer_total')
)

# Window frame
stmt = select(
    Sale.date,
    func.avg(Sale.amount).over(
        partition_by=Sale.product_id,
        order_by=Sale.date,
        rows=(-2, 0)  # Current row and 2 preceding
    )
)
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
let query = select!(User)
    .column(Expr::function("row_number", vec![])
        .over()
        .order_by(User::created_at.asc())
        .as_("row_num")
    );

let query = select!(Order)
    .column(Order::amount.sum()
        .over()
        .partition_by(Order::customer_id)
        .as_("customer_total")
    );

// With frame
let query = select!(Sale)
    .column(Sale::amount.avg()
        .over()
        .partition_by(Sale::product_id)
        .order_by(Sale::date.asc())
        .rows_between(-2, 0)  // ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    );
```

## Window Functions to Support

- ROW_NUMBER()
- RANK()
- DENSE_RANK()
- NTILE(n)
- LAG(expr, offset)
- LEAD(expr, offset)
- FIRST_VALUE(expr)
- LAST_VALUE(expr)
- NTH_VALUE(expr, n)

Plus aggregates with OVER:
- SUM(), AVG(), COUNT(), MIN(), MAX()

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] over() creates window function
- [ ] partition_by() works
- [ ] order_by() works
- [ ] rows_between() for frame
- [ ] range_between() for frame
- [ ] All window functions supported
- [ ] Correct SQL for each dialect

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-query/src/window.rs)
- [ ] Test ROW_NUMBER() OVER generation
- [ ] Test RANK() and DENSE_RANK()
- [ ] Test PARTITION BY clause
- [ ] Test ORDER BY within OVER
- [ ] Test frame specification (ROWS/RANGE)
- [ ] Test LAG/LEAD functions
- [ ] Test aggregate functions in OVER

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/query_window.rs)
- [ ] SELECT with ROW_NUMBER() ranks correctly
- [ ] PARTITION BY groups correctly
- [ ] Running sum with SUM() OVER
- [ ] Complex window: RANK() PARTITION BY x ORDER BY y
- [ ] Multiple window functions in same query
- [ ] Window function in subquery

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: Window clause SQL generation
- [ ] TRACE: Frame specification details
","notes":"DarkStone working on implementing window functions. Starting with core Window struct and OVER clause support.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:07:06.311240310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:18:30.964955236Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:18:30.964888201Z","close_reason":"Implemented window functions with OVER clause support. Added: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, PERCENT_RANK, CUME_DIST, NTILE, LAG, LEAD, FIRST_VALUE, LAST_VALUE, NTH_VALUE. Also added PARTITION BY, ORDER BY within windows, and frame specifications (ROWS/RANGE/GROUPS BETWEEN). All 21 window function tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cy","depends_on_id":"bd-1n7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-39v","title":"Implement change tracking and dirty detection","description":"# Task: Implement Change Tracking and Dirty Detection

## Context
Change tracking detects when a persistent object has been modified since it was loaded from the database. This is essential for the Unit of Work pattern to know which objects need UPDATE statements on flush.

## Detection Strategy
1. **Snapshot comparison**: Store serialized original state, compare on flush
2. **Field-level granularity**: Know which fields changed (for partial updates)
3. **Deep comparison**: Handle nested objects (serde-based)

## What to Implement

### 1. ChangeTracker
\`\`\`rust
use std::collections::HashSet;

/// Tracks changes to objects in the session.
pub struct ChangeTracker {
    /// Original snapshots by object key
    snapshots: HashMap<ObjectKey, ObjectSnapshot>,
}

#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct ObjectSnapshot {
    /// Serialized original state
    data: Vec<u8>,
    /// Timestamp when snapshot was taken
    taken_at: std::time::Instant,
}

impl ChangeTracker {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self { snapshots: HashMap::new() }
    }
    
    /// Take a snapshot of an object.
    pub fn snapshot<T: Model + Serialize>(&mut self, key: ObjectKey, obj: &T) {
        let data = serde_json::to_vec(obj).unwrap_or_default();
        self.snapshots.insert(key, ObjectSnapshot {
            data,
            taken_at: std::time::Instant::now(),
        });
    }
    
    /// Check if an object has changed since its snapshot.
    pub fn is_dirty<T: Model + Serialize>(&self, key: &ObjectKey, obj: &T) -> bool {
        let Some(snapshot) = self.snapshots.get(key) else {
            return true; // No snapshot = treat as dirty
        };
        
        let current = serde_json::to_vec(obj).unwrap_or_default();
        current != snapshot.data
    }
    
    /// Get changed fields between snapshot and current state.
    pub fn changed_fields<T: Model + Serialize>(&self, key: &ObjectKey, obj: &T) -> Vec<&'static str> {
        let Some(snapshot) = self.snapshots.get(key) else {
            return T::fields().iter().map(|f| f.name).collect();
        };
        
        // Parse both as JSON objects and compare fields
        let original: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&snapshot.data)
            .unwrap_or(serde_json::Value::Null);
        let current: serde_json::Value = serde_json::to_value(obj)
            .unwrap_or(serde_json::Value::Null);
        
        let mut changed = Vec::new();
        for field in T::fields() {
            let orig_val = original.get(field.name);
            let curr_val = current.get(field.name);
            if orig_val != curr_val {
                changed.push(field.name);
            }
        }
        changed
    }
    
    /// Clear snapshot (after commit or discard).
    pub fn clear(&mut self, key: &ObjectKey) {
        self.snapshots.remove(key);
    }
    
    /// Clear all snapshots.
    pub fn clear_all(&mut self) {
        self.snapshots.clear();
    }
    
    /// Update snapshot after flush (new baseline).
    pub fn refresh<T: Model + Serialize>(&mut self, key: ObjectKey, obj: &T) {
        self.snapshot(key, obj);
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. DirtySet for Flush
\`\`\`rust
impl ChangeTracker {
    /// Get all dirty objects in the session.
    pub fn dirty_objects(&self, identity_map: &IdentityMap) -> Vec<(ObjectKey, Vec<&'static str>)> {
        let mut dirty = Vec::new();
        
        for (key, tracked) in identity_map.iter() {
            if tracked.state != ObjectState::Persistent {
                continue; // Only check persistent objects
            }
            
            // Type-erase the comparison (using stored original bytes)
            if let Some(snapshot) = self.snapshots.get(key) {
                let current = tracked.serialize_for_comparison();
                if current != snapshot.data {
                    let changed = self.compute_changed_fields(key, &current, &snapshot.data);
                    dirty.push((*key, changed));
                }
            }
        }
        
        dirty
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Partial Update Generation
\`\`\`rust
impl Session {
    /// Generate UPDATE for only changed fields (partial update).
    fn generate_update_for_dirty(&self, key: &ObjectKey, changed_fields: &[&str]) -> Option<PendingOp> {
        let tracked = self.identity_map.get_by_key(key)?;
        
        // Build SET clause for only changed fields
        let set_columns: Vec<&str> = changed_fields.iter()
            .filter(|f| !T::is_primary_key(f))
            .copied()
            .collect();
        
        if set_columns.is_empty() {
            return None; // No non-PK fields changed
        }
        
        let set_values: Vec<Value> = set_columns.iter()
            .map(|col| tracked.get_field_value(col))
            .collect();
        
        Some(PendingOp::Update {
            key: *key,
            table: T::TABLE_NAME,
            pk_columns: T::PRIMARY_KEY.to_vec(),
            pk_values: tracked.primary_key_value(),
            set_columns,
            set_values,
        })
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Comprehensive Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

1. **Snapshot Tests**
   - \`test_snapshot_captures_current_state\`: Serialized bytes stored
   - \`test_snapshot_overwrites_previous\`: New snapshot replaces old
   - \`test_snapshot_timestamp_recorded\`: taken_at is set

2. **Dirty Detection Tests**
   - \`test_is_dirty_false_if_unchanged\`: Same data = not dirty
   - \`test_is_dirty_true_if_field_changed\`: Any field change = dirty
   - \`test_is_dirty_true_if_no_snapshot\`: Missing snapshot = dirty
   - \`test_is_dirty_detects_nested_change\`: Nested object changed

3. **Changed Fields Tests**
   - \`test_changed_fields_empty_if_unchanged\`: No changes = empty vec
   - \`test_changed_fields_lists_modified\`: Returns only changed field names
   - \`test_changed_fields_multiple_changes\`: Multiple fields changed
   - \`test_changed_fields_ignores_unchanged\`: Unchanged fields not included

4. **Clear/Refresh Tests**
   - \`test_clear_removes_snapshot\`: clear() deletes entry
   - \`test_clear_all_removes_all\`: clear_all() empties map
   - \`test_refresh_updates_baseline\`: refresh() takes new snapshot

5. **Dirty Objects Tests**
   - \`test_dirty_objects_returns_all_dirty\`: All dirty objects found
   - \`test_dirty_objects_excludes_clean\`: Clean objects not included
   - \`test_dirty_objects_excludes_new\`: New objects not checked (handled separately)

6. **Partial Update Tests**
   - \`test_partial_update_only_changed\`: SET includes only changed columns
   - \`test_partial_update_excludes_pk\`: PK not in SET clause
   - \`test_partial_update_none_if_pk_only\`: Returns None if only PK changed

### Integration Tests

1. **With Session**
   - \`test_session_snapshots_on_load\`: get() takes snapshot
   - \`test_session_detects_dirty_on_flush\`: flush() finds dirty objects
   - \`test_session_updates_only_dirty\`: Clean objects not UPDATEd

2. **With Real Database**
   - \`test_change_tracking_persists_modifications\`: Modified fields saved
   - \`test_change_tracking_partial_update_correct\`: Only changed columns in SQL

### E2E Test Script

\`\`\`rust
/// E2E: Change tracking detects modifications
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_change_tracking_workflow() {
    let pool = setup_test_pool().await;
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    // Load object (snapshot taken)
    let mut hero = session.get::<Hero>(1).await.unwrap().unwrap();
    tracing::debug!(name = %hero.name, "Loaded hero");
    
    // Verify not dirty initially
    assert!(!session.is_dirty(&hero));
    
    // Modify object
    hero.name = "New Name".into();
    
    // Now dirty
    assert!(session.is_dirty(&hero));
    let changed = session.changed_fields(&hero);
    assert!(changed.contains(&"name"));
    tracing::info!(changed = ?changed, "Changed fields detected");
    
    // Flush generates UPDATE
    session.flush().await.unwrap();
    
    // After flush, new baseline taken
    assert!(!session.is_dirty(&hero));
    
    // Verify DB updated
    let db_hero: Hero = query_hero_by_id(&pool, 1).await;
    assert_eq!(db_hero.name, "New Name");
}

/// E2E: Partial UPDATE only touches changed columns
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_partial_update() {
    let pool = setup_test_pool().await;
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    // Load hero with many fields
    let mut hero = session.get::<Hero>(1).await.unwrap().unwrap();
    
    // Change only name (not age, team_id, etc.)
    hero.name = "Updated Name".into();
    
    // Capture SQL
    let sql_log = enable_sql_logging(&session);
    
    session.flush().await.unwrap();
    
    // Verify SQL only updates name column
    let update_sql = sql_log.last_update();
    assert!(update_sql.contains("SET name ="));
    assert!(!update_sql.contains("age"));
    assert!(!update_sql.contains("team_id"));
    
    tracing::info!(sql = %update_sql, "Partial UPDATE generated");
}

/// E2E: No update if no changes
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_no_update_if_clean() {
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    // Load and don't modify
    let hero = session.get::<Hero>(1).await.unwrap().unwrap();
    
    let query_count_before = get_query_count(&pool);
    
    // Flush should do nothing
    session.flush().await.unwrap();
    
    let query_count_after = get_query_count(&pool);
    
    // No UPDATE executed
    assert_eq!(query_count_before, query_count_after);
}
\`\`\`

\`\`\`rust
impl ChangeTracker {
    #[tracing::instrument(level = "trace", skip(self, obj))]
    pub fn snapshot<T: Model + Serialize>(&mut self, key: ObjectKey, obj: &T) {
        let size = serde_json::to_vec(obj).map(|v| v.len()).unwrap_or(0);
        tracing::trace!(
            model = std::any::type_name::<T>(),
            pk_hash = key.pk_hash,
            snapshot_bytes = size,
            "Taking object snapshot"
        );
        // ...
    }
    
    pub fn is_dirty<T: Model + Serialize>(&self, key: &ObjectKey, obj: &T) -> bool {
        let dirty = /* ... */;
        tracing::trace!(
            pk_hash = key.pk_hash,
            dirty = dirty,
            "Dirty check result"
        );
        dirty
    }
    
    pub fn changed_fields<T: Model + Serialize>(&self, key: &ObjectKey, obj: &T) -> Vec<&'static str> {
        let fields = /* ... */;
        tracing::debug!(
            model = std::any::type_name::<T>(),
            changed_count = fields.len(),
            fields = ?fields,
            "Detected changed fields"
        );
        fields
    }
}

 {"id":"bd-3adf","title":"Implement model_dump_json()","description":"## Description

Serialize model to JSON string.

## Python Signature

```python
def model_dump_json(
    self,
    *,
    indent: int | None = None,
    include: set[str] | None = None,
    exclude: set[str] | None = None,
    context: dict | None = None,
    by_alias: bool = False,
    exclude_unset: bool = False,
    exclude_defaults: bool = False,
    exclude_none: bool = False,
    exclude_computed_fields: bool = False,
    round_trip: bool = False,
    warnings: bool = True,
) -> str:
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
impl<M: Model> ModelDumpJson for M {
    fn model_dump_json(&self, options: DumpJsonOptions) -> Result<String, Error> {
        let dict = self.model_dump(options.into());
        match options.indent {
            Some(indent) => serde_json::to_string_pretty(&dict),
            None => serde_json::to_string(&dict),
        }
    }
}
```

-{"id":"bd-3bmd","title":"Joined-table inheritance DML: explicit WHERE/SET + ON CONFLICT semantics","description":"## Problem

Joined-table inheritance DML now works for model-based insert/update/delete, but advanced builder features are not supported (explicit WHERE, explicit SET, ON CONFLICT/UPSERT, RETURNING shape across base+child).

## Goals
- Define consistent semantics for joined inheritance DML when users use:
  - `UpdateBuilder::empty().set(...).filter(...)`
  - `DeleteBuilder::new().filter(...)`
  - `InsertBuilder::on_conflict_*`
  - `execute_returning` (what row shape is returned?)
- Implement the behaviors and add unit tests + SQLite integration coverage.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Joined-child UPDATE supports explicit WHERE/SET without ambiguity (document table targeting)
- [ ] Joined-child DELETE supports explicit WHERE (child-only vs base+child; documented)
- [ ] Joined-child INSERT supports ON CONFLICT semantics (document strategy)
- [ ] Clear error messages for unsupported combinations
- [ ] Tests for SQL generation and behavior

 {"id":"bd-3dw4","title":"Implement Enum type complete support","description":"## Description

Full enum type support across all dialects.

## Python Behavior

```python
from enum import Enum

class Status(str, Enum):
    ACTIVE = 'active'
    INACTIVE = 'inactive'
    PENDING = 'pending'

class User(SQLModel, table=True):
    status: Status
```

## SQL Generation

- PostgreSQL: CREATE TYPE status AS ENUM ('active', 'inactive', 'pending')
- MySQL: ENUM('active', 'inactive', 'pending')
- SQLite: TEXT with CHECK constraint

## Rust Implementation

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, SqlEnum)]
pub enum Status {
    Active,
    Inactive,
    Pending,
}

#[derive(Model)]
struct User {
    status: Status,
}
```

### SqlEnum Derive

Generates:
- ToSql/FromSql implementations
- CREATE TYPE DDL
- CHECK constraints for SQLite

- [ ] PostgreSQL CREATE TYPE
- [ ] MySQL ENUM()
- [ ] SQLite CHECK constraint
- [ ] Serialization (string values)

 {"id":"bd-3en","title":"Implement schema diff engine","description":"# Task: Implement Schema Diff Engine

## Context
The schema diff engine compares two database schemas (current DB state vs desired Model state) and produces a list of operations needed to migrate from one to the other.

## Design Philosophy
1. **Safe by default**: Destructive operations require explicit confirmation
2. **Reversible**: Each operation has an inverse for rollback
3. **Ordered**: Operations sorted for FK constraint safety
4. **Database-agnostic**: Same diff logic for SQLite/MySQL/Postgres

## What to Implement

### 1. Schema Representation
\`\`\`rust
/// Represents a database table schema.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct TableSchema {
    pub name: String,
    pub columns: Vec<ColumnSchema>,
    pub primary_key: Vec<String>,
    pub indexes: Vec<IndexSchema>,
    pub foreign_keys: Vec<ForeignKeySchema>,
}

/// Represents a column in a table.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ColumnSchema {
    pub name: String,
    pub sql_type: String,
    pub nullable: bool,
    pub default: Option<String>,
    pub is_auto_increment: bool,
}

/// Represents an index.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct IndexSchema {
    pub name: String,
    pub columns: Vec<String>,
    pub unique: bool,
}

/// Represents a foreign key constraint.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ForeignKeySchema {
    pub name: Option<String>,
    pub columns: Vec<String>,
    pub referenced_table: String,
    pub referenced_columns: Vec<String>,
    pub on_delete: ReferentialAction,
    pub on_update: ReferentialAction,
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Schema Operations
\`\`\`rust
/// A single schema change operation.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum SchemaOp {
    // Table operations
    CreateTable(TableSchema),
    DropTable { name: String, cascade: bool },
    RenameTable { from: String, to: String },
    
    // Column operations
    AddColumn { table: String, column: ColumnSchema },
    DropColumn { table: String, column: String },
    AlterColumn { table: String, from: ColumnSchema, to: ColumnSchema },
    RenameColumn { table: String, from: String, to: String },
    
    // Index operations
    CreateIndex { table: String, index: IndexSchema },
    DropIndex { table: String, name: String },
    
    // Foreign key operations
    AddForeignKey { table: String, fk: ForeignKeySchema },
    DropForeignKey { table: String, name: String },
}

impl SchemaOp {
    /// Get the inverse operation for rollback.
    pub fn inverse(&self) -> Option<SchemaOp> {
        match self {
            SchemaOp::CreateTable(t) => Some(SchemaOp::DropTable { 
                name: t.name.clone(), 
                cascade: false 
            }),
            SchemaOp::DropTable { name, .. } => None, // Can't reverse without data
            SchemaOp::AddColumn { table, column } => Some(SchemaOp::DropColumn {
                table: table.clone(),
                column: column.name.clone(),
            }),
            // ... etc
        }
    }
    
    /// Is this operation destructive (loses data)?
    pub fn is_destructive(&self) -> bool {
        matches!(self, 
            SchemaOp::DropTable { .. } |
            SchemaOp::DropColumn { .. } |
            SchemaOp::AlterColumn { .. } // May lose data on type change
        )
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Schema Differ
\`\`\`rust
pub struct SchemaDiffer {
    /// How to handle destructive operations
    pub destructive_policy: DestructivePolicy,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum DestructivePolicy {
    /// Skip destructive operations
    Skip,
    /// Include but mark as needing confirmation
    Warn,
    /// Include all operations
    Allow,
}

impl SchemaDiffer {
    pub fn diff(&self, current: &[TableSchema], desired: &[TableSchema]) -> SchemaDiff {
        let mut ops = Vec::new();
        
        // Build lookup maps
        let current_tables: HashMap<&str, &TableSchema> = ...;
        let desired_tables: HashMap<&str, &TableSchema> = ...;
        
        // Find new tables
        for (name, schema) in &desired_tables {
            if !current_tables.contains_key(name) {
                ops.push(SchemaOp::CreateTable((*schema).clone()));
            }
        }
        
        // Find dropped tables
        for (name, _) in &current_tables {
            if !desired_tables.contains_key(name) {
                if self.destructive_policy != DestructivePolicy::Skip {
                    ops.push(SchemaOp::DropTable { 
                        name: name.to_string(), 
                        cascade: false 
                    });
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Find modified tables
        for (name, desired) in &desired_tables {
            if let Some(current) = current_tables.get(name) {
                ops.extend(self.diff_table(current, desired));
            }
        }
        
        SchemaDiff { ops: self.order_operations(ops) }
    }
    
    fn diff_table(&self, current: &TableSchema, desired: &TableSchema) -> Vec<SchemaOp> {
        // Compare columns, indexes, foreign keys
        // ...
    }
    
    fn order_operations(&self, ops: Vec<SchemaOp>) -> Vec<SchemaOp> {
        // Sort: drop FK -> drop index -> drop/alter columns -> create columns -> create index -> create FK
        // ...
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 4. Schema Diff Result
\`\`\`rust
pub struct SchemaDiff {
    pub ops: Vec<SchemaOp>,
}

impl SchemaDiff {
    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool { self.ops.is_empty() }
    
    pub fn has_destructive(&self) -> bool {
        self.ops.iter().any(|op| op.is_destructive())
    }
    
    pub fn destructive_ops(&self) -> Vec<&SchemaOp> {
        self.ops.iter().filter(|op| op.is_destructive()).collect()
    }
    
    /// Generate human-readable summary.
    pub fn summary(&self) -> String {
        let creates = self.ops.iter().filter(|o| matches!(o, SchemaOp::CreateTable(_))).count();
        let drops = self.ops.iter().filter(|o| matches!(o, SchemaOp::DropTable { .. })).count();
        // ...
        format!("{} operations: {} creates, {} drops, ...", self.ops.len(), creates, drops)
    }
}

## Comprehensive Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

1. **Table Diff Tests**
   - \`test_diff_new_table_creates\`: Missing table -> CreateTable op
   - \`test_diff_dropped_table_drops\`: Extra table -> DropTable op
   - \`test_diff_identical_tables_empty\`: Same schema -> no ops
   - \`test_diff_renamed_table_detects\`: Different name, same structure -> RenameTable

2. **Column Diff Tests**
   - \`test_diff_new_column_adds\`: Missing column -> AddColumn
   - \`test_diff_dropped_column_drops\`: Extra column -> DropColumn
   - \`test_diff_type_change_alters\`: VARCHAR(50) -> VARCHAR(100) -> AlterColumn
   - \`test_diff_nullable_change_alters\`: NOT NULL -> NULL -> AlterColumn
   - \`test_diff_default_change_alters\`: DEFAULT changes -> AlterColumn

3. **Index Diff Tests**
   - \`test_diff_new_index_creates\`: CreateIndex op generated
   - \`test_diff_dropped_index_drops\`: DropIndex op generated
   - \`test_diff_index_columns_changed\`: Drop + Create

4. **Foreign Key Diff Tests**
   - \`test_diff_new_fk_adds\`: AddForeignKey op
   - \`test_diff_dropped_fk_drops\`: DropForeignKey op
   - \`test_diff_fk_action_changed\`: Drop + Add for referential action change

5. **Operation Ordering Tests**
   - \`test_ordering_drops_fk_before_column\`: FK dropped before column
   - \`test_ordering_creates_table_before_fk\`: Table exists before FK references it
   - \`test_ordering_drops_index_before_column\`: Index dropped before column it references

6. **Policy Tests**
   - \`test_policy_skip_excludes_destructive\`: Skip policy filters drops
   - \`test_policy_warn_includes_but_marks\`: Warn policy includes with flag
   - \`test_policy_allow_includes_all\`: Allow policy includes everything

7. **Inverse Operation Tests**
   - \`test_inverse_create_table_is_drop\`: CreateTable.inverse() = DropTable
   - \`test_inverse_add_column_is_drop\`: AddColumn.inverse() = DropColumn
   - \`test_inverse_drop_table_is_none\`: Can't reverse data loss

### Integration Tests

1. **Full Schema Comparison**
   - \`test_diff_complex_schema_change\`: Multiple tables, columns, indexes, FKs
   - \`test_diff_real_model_vs_db\`: Compare Model-derived schema to actual DB

2. **Edge Cases**
   - \`test_diff_empty_to_populated\`: Fresh DB -> full schema
   - \`test_diff_populated_to_empty\`: Full schema -> drop all (with policy)
   - \`test_diff_circular_fk_dependencies\`: Tables referencing each other

### E2E Test Script

\`\`\`rust
/// E2E: Schema diff workflow
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_schema_diff_to_migration() {
    let pool = setup_test_pool().await;
    
    // Get current DB schema
    let introspector = SchemaIntrospector::new(&pool);
    let current = introspector.introspect().await.unwrap();
    tracing::info!(tables = current.len(), "Introspected current schema");
    
    // Get desired schema from Models
    let desired = SchemaExtractor::extract::<(Hero, Team, Power)>();
    tracing::info!(tables = desired.len(), "Extracted desired schema");
    
    // Compute diff
    let differ = SchemaDiffer { destructive_policy: DestructivePolicy::Warn };
    let diff = differ.diff(&current, &desired);
    
    tracing::info!(
        ops = diff.ops.len(),
        destructive = diff.has_destructive(),
        summary = %diff.summary(),
        "Schema diff computed"
    );
    
    // Log each operation
    for op in &diff.ops {
        tracing::debug!(op = ?op, destructive = op.is_destructive(), "Schema operation");
    }
    
    assert!(diff.ops.len() > 0, "Expected some schema changes");
}

/// E2E: Verify operation ordering prevents FK violations
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_operation_ordering_fk_safe() {
    // Schema with tables A -> B (A has FK to B)
    // Add both tables from empty
    // Verify CreateTable(B) comes before AddForeignKey(A.b_id)
}

+## 0. Current Focus (2026-02-10): bd-3bmd (joined-table inheritance DML: explicit WHERE/SET + ON CONFLICT)
+
+### 0.1 UPDATE: Explicit WHERE/SET
+- [ ] Support `UpdateBuilder::empty().set(...).filter(...)` for joined children by splitting SET columns across parent/child tables
+- [ ] Support `UpdateBuilder::new(&model).set(...).filter(...)` (explicit SET overrides model values)
+- [ ] When `.filter(...)` is present, apply it via `WHERE (pk...) IN (SELECT pk... FROM child JOIN parent WHERE <filter>)` so the predicate can reference either table
+- [ ] Define/implement behavior for `.set_only(...)` to apply to parent columns as well (when names match parent fields)
+
+### 0.2 DELETE: Explicit WHERE (+ RETURNING)
+- [ ] Support `DeleteBuilder::new().filter(...)` for joined children: select PKs first, then delete child rows, then parent rows (same tx)
+- [ ] Support `DeleteBuilder::...returning().execute_returning(...)` for joined children by selecting joined rows before delete (tx)
+
+### 0.3 INSERT: ON CONFLICT (+ RETURNING)
+- [ ] Support `InsertBuilder::on_conflict_*` for joined children with conservative rules:
+- [ ] Require explicit PK values (no auto-increment PK + upsert yet)
+- [ ] Apply ON CONFLICT for both parent insert and child insert inside a single tx
+- [ ] Make `execute_returning` return a joined row shape (base + child columns, `table__col` aliases) using a follow-up SELECT in-tx
+
+### 0.4 Tests (SQLite, end-to-end)
+- [ ] Add integration test `crates/sqlmodel/tests/joined_inheritance_dml_advanced_sqlite.rs`:
+- [ ] UPDATE with explicit SET + filter updates both parent and child tables
+- [ ] DELETE with explicit filter deletes from both tables
+- [ ] INSERT with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE updates both tables (explicit PK models)
+- [ ] RETURNING for joined child includes both `child__*` and `parent__*` columns
+
